description: The synthetic-data generation experiments that produce data_synth/. Default reproduction uses the committed batches; rerun via reproduce_synth.py only when investigating prompt/model variation. Each step requires OPENROUTER_API_KEY in the environment.

steps.description: ST1/ST2 contrastive-pair batch (130 pairs across the eight ST2 fallacy types). Backs §3.4 of the paper.
pe-targeted batch (50 per label, openai/gpt-4o-mini, T=0.9). Backs Appendix A Prompt 3.

pseudo_labels.description: Pseudo-labelling does not use an LLM API. The shared/pseudo_label.py CLI takes a trained checkpoint and emits top-K confident self-predictions on the unlabelled pool. Each committed batch was produced from the matching real-only enhanced checkpoint (recorded in each record's source_exp field): pseudo_v001_st3 from exp_0066, pseudo_v001_st2 from exp_0065. NB the effective confidence floor is not a hard tau=0.9: pseudo_label.py:167-172 relaxes the floor to 0 for under-predicted classes, so the committed pseudo_v001_st3 batch contains some sub-0.9 records. NB also the candidate pool is the full unlabelled set, not pre-filtered to non-fallacious; for st3 the script stamps fallacy_exists=0 on every selected record (pseudo_label.py:208-214).
